symptoms.symptom_name: Non-draining indwelling catheter with urine bypassing around it
symptoms.character: leg bag empty; foul-smelling urine leaking around the tube

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical obstruction of an infected system is the pivotal feature separating this from a simple UTI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe colicky suprapubic pain with bursting/full sensation
symptoms.character: colicky, central lower abdominal, over the bladder

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension pain from a bladder that cannot empty behind the block.
Central suprapubic location correlating with catheter failure argues against right iliac fossa appendicitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with rigors (uncontrollable shaking, freezing cold)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rigors indicate bacteraemia/systemic sepsis rather than localised infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constipation — no bowel motion for 4 days, faecal loading

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Faecal loading externally compresses the catheter and precipitates the occlusion; also a modifiable precipitant.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-obtainable objective fever; admissible in telehealth but unverified (home device).

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent urinary tract infections on a long-term indwelling catheter (neurogenic bladder)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic catheter colonisation predisposes to encrustation, blockage and recurrent infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness on sitting up (patient-reportable proxy for circulatory compromise)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural lightheadedness with sepsis suggests early distributive shock; the telehealth-visible marker of haemodynamic compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: No headache, vomiting or altered mental state; shunt tract non-tender

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of raised-ICP / shunt-sepsis features makes shunt infection unlikely, localising the source to the bladder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline hypotension and tachycardia (BP 100/60, HR 125) with SIRS

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic evidence of sepsis with early shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers this from lightheadedness, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended, exquisitely tender bladder palpable to the umbilicus; firm faecal loading LLQ

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms mechanical retention behind the block — clinician palpation only; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Occluded 16Fr Foley with purulent, foul-smelling urine bypassing the meatus

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Obstructive catheter-associated UTI with impending urosepsis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Purulent bypass around an occluded catheter confirms an obstructed infected system — clinician exam; sealed.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Obstructed infected urinary tract with sepsis
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Catheter-associated obstructive urinary tract infection with impending urosepsis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A non-draining catheter + suprapubic distension pain + rigors + postural lightheadedness together define a urological sepsis emergency, distinct from a simple recurrent UTI.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate catheter change with drainage of residual (source control) — in facility
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Relieving the obstruction is both diagnostic and therapeutic; cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood cultures, lactate, FBC/CRP, urine microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm sepsis severity and guide targeted antibiotics; facility-based, not available remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient renal ultrasound in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do not delay emergency escalation to arrange non-urgent imaging; source control and resuscitation come first.
